description: Trigger discrimination: frontend perf DESIGN (this skill) vs measurement mechanics (performance-test-harness), build correctness (vite-build-qa-engineer), shared data caching (caching-strategy-designer), the end-to-end split (latency-budget-architect), and functional E2E (playwright-e2e-engineer).

cases.prompt: The product feels heavy in the browser: big bundle, slow first load on phones, janky lists. Produce the optimization plan and the budgets that keep it fixed.
cases.reason: Bundle strategy, rendering path, runtime patterns, and budget values are the frontend perf DESIGN surface. The harness later measures; the build QA later verifies chunks; no functional tests are requested.

cases.prompt: Set up the lab measurement that runs our key pages on a throttled mobile profile every release, keeps percentile baselines, and fails CI on regression.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (designs-vs-measures) — running pages under a profile, baselines, and CI regression gates are the measurement INSTRUMENT. The perf engineer defines what to measure and the budget numbers; the harness owns how measurement executes and gates.
cases.reason: Instrument construction and gate mechanics, not optimization design.

cases.prompt: After our chunk-splitting change, the staging build 404s on a lazy chunk and an env-specific import breaks. Verify and fix the build pipeline behavior.
cases.reason: Broken chunks and env-specific build failures are build CORRECTNESS QA — vite-build-qa-engineer's scope. The perf engineer reshapes the bundle for speed and composes that skill to keep the reshaped build verified.

cases.prompt: Design caching for our API responses at the CDN edge — which endpoints, what keys and TTLs, and how invalidation works on updates.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — caching split: shared HTTP/data response caching (keys, TTLs, invalidation, envelopes) is the cache designer's; the frontend skill's caching scope is the browser ASSET path (hashed immutable bundles, service-worker strategy).
cases.reason: API response caching with invalidation design is data-cache craft, not asset-pipeline work.

cases.prompt: Page load is 3 seconds total — how many milliseconds belong to the API, the gateway, and the browser respectively, and who owns what share of the 3 seconds?
cases.reason: Allocating an end-to-end number across hops — including the frontend's SHARE — is the budget architect's table. The frontend engineer takes its allocated share and designs how the browser meets it.
